title: Cacalia ainsliaeiflora
taxonomy: Cacalia ainsliaeiflora is a plant species classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the tribe Cardueae and the genus Cacalia. The classification of Cacalia ainsliaeiflora is based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features. The species is also known to be endemic to certain regions. Further classification details may be subject to ongoing botanical research and updates.

properties: Cacalia ainsliaeiflora is reported to have various therapeutic properties, primarily derived from its traditional use in medicine. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant is also said to possess immunomodulatory and anticancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Additionally, Cacalia ainsliaeiflora is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the plant's therapeutic potential and its application in modern medicine.
intro: Cacalia ainsliaeiflora, also known as Korean field thistle, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia ainsliaeiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Cacalia ainsliaeiflora is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
